methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
ADASI is a sovereign-funded defense and security company at the EDGE stage backed by the EDGE Group. The valuation universe comprises 10 comparable companies: 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. However, the narrow peer set presents material analytical constraints.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The Defense & Security sector contains 21 same-sector comparables, but none satisfy the narrowing criteria required for robust triangulation. Zero companies matched on same-stage, zero on adjacent-stage, and zero on similar-valuation-tier filters. This fragmentation reflects the heterogeneous nature of defense technology, where companies span diverse subsectors, funding structures, and revenue maturity. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable selection bias and obscure valuation drivers specific to ADASI's positioning.

The lack of sufficient narrow peers prevents standard triangulation. Any valuation estimate at this stage would rest on qualitative assessments of ADASI's competitive positioning, sovereign funding advantages, and the macroeconomic tailwinds in defense spending—factors outside the scope of comparable-company analysis. Further data on ADASI's revenue, profit margins, and growth rates, combined with closer characterization of the five named private peers, would be necessary to establish a defensible range. Until such data materializes, quantitative valuation synthesis remains premature.
